overview.tagline: The Borough of Trees — a historic, walkable community with small-town elegance
overview.description: Rutherford, known as the 'Borough of Trees,' is a charming, historic community in southern Bergen County that offers a rare combination of walkability, architectural beauty, and convenient access to New York City. The borough features a vibrant downtown along Park Avenue, stunning Victorian and Edwardian homes, and an engaged community that values its heritage. Home to Felician University and the acclaimed Williams Center for the Arts, Rutherford attracts families, young professionals, and those seeking a cultured suburban lifestyle just minutes from the Meadowlands and Manhattan.
highlights: Known as the 'Borough of Trees' for its magnificent tree-lined streets
Walkable Park Avenue downtown with shops, restaurants, and cafes
Home to Felician University and the Williams Center for the Arts
NJ Transit rail service and bus routes to NYC

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Rutherford features a diverse housing stock including Victorian homes, colonials, split-levels, and condominiums in both historic and newer developments.
realEstate.recentTrends: Rutherford has seen strong buyer interest driven by its walkability, NYC commuter convenience, and historic charm. Home prices have appreciated significantly, especially for well-maintained Victorian and Edwardian homes near the downtown. The condo market has also been active with new developments adding inventory.
neighborhoodNotes: Park Avenue area — Walking distance to downtown shops, restaurants, and the train station
The Hutton Park neighborhood — Stately homes surrounding the park, one of the most desirable areas
West End — Mix of colonials and split-levels with a quieter residential feel
East Rutherford border — More affordable homes with Meadowlands access
Ridge Road area — Mix of residential and commercial properties

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Rutherford Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same recycling day
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through December
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Annual water quality reports available online
Emergency water repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement. Historic preservation guidelines may apply.
details: Building permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Historic preservation considerations for properties in certain districts
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates commuter parking, downtown municipal lots, and metered street parking.
details: Commuter parking permits available for train station lots
Metered parking along Park Avenue downtown
Municipal parking lots near the downtown shopping district
ParkMobile app accepted at metered spaces
Overnight parking restrictions enforced on borough streets

additionalServices.title: Rutherford Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library in a beautiful building offering extensive collections, programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 150 Park Avenue
Children's story times, teen programs, and adult events
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and meeting rooms
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Historic building near the center of downtown

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer camp programs
Adult fitness and wellness programs
Senior citizen programs and trips
Seasonal events at Memorial Park and Hutton Park

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Rutherford enforces noise regulations to preserve the residential character of its historic neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to certain hours
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties, preserving the borough's attractive streetscapes.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept under 10 inches
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed
Historic properties should maintain architectural integrity where applicable

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Rutherford requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough, especially in the downtown.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM without permit
Metered parking in downtown zone — 2-hour limit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons prohibited on residential streets
Commuter lots require valid borough permits

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with borough housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Short-term rentals (under 30 days) are not permitted in residential zones
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Historic Preservation
other.summary: Rutherford has guidelines for preserving the character of its historic residential architecture.
keyRules: Properties in designated historic zones may have additional review requirements
Exterior alterations to historic properties should be sympathetic to original architecture
The Historic Preservation Commission reviews applications as needed
Demolition of historically significant structures may face additional scrutiny

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: As the 'Borough of Trees,' Rutherford has strong protections for its tree canopy.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees over a specified diameter on private property
Borough Shade Tree Commission oversees public tree management
Replacement planting may be required when significant trees are removed
Damage to borough shade trees subject to fines

publicSchools.name: Lincoln Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the eastern section of the borough

publicSchools.name: Washington Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the western section

publicSchools.name: Union Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the central section

publicSchools.name: Pierrepont School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school serving all borough students in grades 6-8

publicSchools.name: Rutherford High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school with a range of academic and extracurricular programs

privateSchools.name: St. Mary High School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic high school in the Archdiocese of Newark

privateSchools.name: St. Mary Elementary School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic elementary school affiliated with St. Mary Church

whyMoveHere: Stunning Victorian and Edwardian architecture on tree-lined streets
Walkable downtown with independently owned shops, restaurants, and cafes
NJ Transit train service and bus routes for easy NYC commuting
Strong community with active civic and cultural organizations
Home to Felician University and the Williams Center for the Arts
Beautiful parks including Hutton Park and Memorial Park

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bergen County Line / Bus
commuteToNYC.duration: 30-45 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Rutherford Station (on Erie Avenue near Park Avenue)
commuteToNYC.notes: The Rutherford train station is centrally located near downtown. Trains run on the Bergen County Line to Hoboken Terminal, with connections to Manhattan via PATH or ferry. Bus routes 190 and 191 provide direct service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. The Lincoln Tunnel is also accessible via Route 3.

costOfLiving.summary: Rutherford offers moderate costs by Bergen County standards, with historic character and walkability that provide strong value. Property taxes are on the higher side.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 20% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Beautiful historic homes and tree-lined streets
Convenient NJ Transit train and bus service to NYC
Active cultural scene with Williams Center and community events
Strong sense of community and civic pride
cons: Property taxes are high relative to home prices
Train commute requires transfer at Hoboken for Manhattan
Some flooding concerns in low-lying areas near the Meadowlands
Downtown parking can be tight during peak times
Proximity to the Meadowlands can mean occasional odors or traffic

restaurants.name: Café Matisse
restaurants.description: Award-winning fine dining restaurant offering creative, globally inspired cuisine in a beautifully renovated firehouse. One of Bergen County's premier dining destinations.

restaurants.name: Mambo Italiano
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant and bar serving classic pasta dishes, brick-oven pizza, and Italian-American favorites in a lively setting.

restaurants.name: Volare's
restaurants.description: Popular Italian restaurant offering traditional red-sauce Italian dishes and a warm, family-friendly atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Village Gourmet
restaurants.description: Gourmet deli and cafe offering sandwiches, salads, prepared foods, and specialty coffee in a cozy downtown setting.

restaurants.name: Noches de Colombia
restaurants.description: Colombian restaurant serving authentic dishes including bandeja paisa, empanadas, and arepas.

parks.name: Hutton Park
parks.description: Beautiful, tree-filled park in the center of a prestigious residential neighborhood, featuring walking paths, benches, and a serene setting ideal for relaxation.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Major borough park with baseball fields, tennis courts, basketball courts, a bandshell for concerts, and memorial installations.

parks.name: Lincoln Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground equipment, open green space, and benches in a family-friendly setting.

parks.name: Tamblyn Field & Civic Center
parks.description: Multi-use field and civic center used for community events, sports, and recreational activities.

events.name: Rutherford Labor Day Street Fair
events.description: One of the largest street fairs in Bergen County, featuring hundreds of vendors, food, live entertainment, and activities spanning Park Avenue.

events.name: Rutherford Multicultural Festival
events.description: Annual celebration of cultural diversity with food, music, dance, and performances from around the world.

events.name: Holiday Stroll & Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration on Park Avenue with tree lighting, caroling, special shopping events, and Santa visits.

events.name: Summer Concert Series at Memorial Park
events.description: Free outdoor concerts held at the Memorial Park bandshell throughout the summer months.

attractions.name: Park Avenue Downtown
attractions.description: Rutherford's charming main street lined with independently owned boutiques, restaurants, cafes, and professional services. The tree-lined avenue is the heart of the community.

attractions.name: Williams Center for the Arts
attractions.description: Cultural venue housed in a restored theater, offering films, live performances, community events, and arts programming.

attractions.name: Felician University
attractions.description: Private Franciscan university located in Rutherford, with a scenic campus, art galleries, and community events open to the public.

shoppingAreas: Park Avenue — Downtown boutiques, restaurants, cafes, and services
Station Square — Shops near the train station
Ridge Road — Additional retail and services
American Dream (East Rutherford) — Major entertainment and shopping complex nearby
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Regional shopping center nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 2nd and 4th Monday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 176 Park Avenue, Rutherford, NJ 07070

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
